=== solid_white
# Full white canvas → all cells render as solid block █.
mode: canvas
width: 4
height: 2
--- js
var Game = {
    init: function(s) {},
    renderCanvas: function(ctx, playerID, w, h) {
        ctx.fillStyle = "#ffffff";
        ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
    }
};
---
===
████
████

=== solid_black
# Full black canvas → after ANSI stripping still shows block chars (color info dropped).
mode: canvas
width: 4
height: 2
--- js
var Game = {
    init: function(s) {},
    renderCanvas: function(ctx, playerID, w, h) {
        ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
        ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
    }
};
---
===
████
████

=== top_half_white
# White top half, black bottom half — exercises the quadrant image pipeline.
# h/2 split at pixel level aligns with cell boundaries (each cell = 2 pixel rows),
# so cell row 0 is all-white and cell row 1 is all-black; both render as solid █.
mode: canvas
width: 4
height: 2
--- js
var Game = {
    init: function(s) {},
    renderCanvas: function(ctx, playerID, w, h) {
        ctx.fillStyle = "#ffffff";
        ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h / 2);
        ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
        ctx.fillRect(0, h / 2, w, h / 2);
    }
};
---
===
████
████
